title: chamaecytisus austriacus subsp. heuffelii
intro: Chamaecytisus austriacus subsp. heuffelii, also known as Heuffel's golden shrub or Heuffel's broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Heuffel's golden shrub include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
